input.content:
Report the quantitative estimate of drug-likeness (QED) of this molecule.

Cc1ccc(C)c(OCC(=O)N2CCN(C(=O)[C@@H]3CCCO3)CC2)c1

Respond with a single floating point number and nothing else.

What is the number of valence electrons in total (including hydrogens) in this compound? Give your answer as an integer.

CC[C@H](C)[C@H](NC(=O)[C@H](CCCNC(=N)N)NC(=O)[C@H](CC(C)C)NC(=O)[C@H](Cc1ccccc1)NC(=O)CN)C(=O)N[C@@H](CCCNC(=N)N)C(=O)N1CCC[C@H]1C(=O)N[C@@H](CCCCN)C(=O)N[C@@H](CC(C)C)C(=O)N[C@@H](CCCCN)C(=O)O

Respond with a single integer and nothing else.

Is this molecule compliant with Lipinski's Rule of Five (MW ≤ 500, HBD ≤ 5, HBA ≤ 10, logP ≤ 5)?

COc1ccc(C(=O)c2c(C)n(CC[N+]3(C)CCOCC3)c3ccccc23)cc1

Respond with 0 or 1 and nothing else. (1 = yes/passes, 0 = no/fails)

Does this molecule contain an amide group (C(=O)N)?

CC(C)c1sc(N2CCN(C)CC2)nc1C(=O)Nc1ccc(F)cc1C(F)(F)F.O=C(O)C(F)(F)F

Respond with 0 or 1 and nothing else. (1 = yes/present, 0 = no/absent)

How many aliphatic carboxylic acid groups (COOH) in this chemical structure?

N[C@@H](Cn1ncc(=O)[nH]c1=O)C(=O)O

Respond with a single integer and nothing else.
